channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Apple finally lets you merge two Apple IDs—kinda. Here’s how
item.description: A few years ago, I chronicled the journey I went on to manually merge two Apple ID accounts into one.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: This Asus Mini PC can do something spooky - automatically detect and wake itself up when someone's approaching
item.description: Asus NUC 15 Pro is a compact powerhouse built for AI, multitasking, and high-resolution work with Intel’s latest Core Ultra processors and Arc graphics.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: OpenAI board unanimously rejects Elon Musk's $97.4 billion proposal
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — OpenAI says its board of directors has unanimously rejected a $97.4 billion takeover bid by Elon Musk. “OpenAI is not for sale, and the board has unanimously rejected Mr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: This DARPA-backed startup banked $100 million for its energy-slashing analog chips
item.description: A young DARPA-backed startup with a fresh spin on a low-power computer chip has raised over $100 million in a Series B funding round, a sign of the wild appetite for more energy-efficient ways to build bigger and better AI.
item.dc:creator: Alex Pasternack, Fast Company

item.title: Photonics Unveils World's Largest Mobile LED Screen
item.description: Discover the JUMBO162, a massive 17 x 9.5-metre 4K display that can withstand wind speeds of up to 61 km/h.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Just 32 percent in US trust AI: Study
item.description: Only about a third of Americans trust artificial intelligence (AI), according to new data from the Edelman Trust Barometer. Just 32 percent of people in the U.S. said they trust the rapidly developing technology, according to the annual global survey’s “Insights for the Technology Sector.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: The NBA's tech summit turns 25 this year, and its return to the Bay Area is a full-circle moment
item.description: NBA Commissioner Adam Silver had no idea 25 years ago that the Tech Summit would turn into much of anything, much less one of the hottest tickets at All-Star weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tim Reynolds, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Zacks Investment hit in data breach - 12 million users potentially at risk
item.description: The company hasn't commented on the allegations yet, but users may wish to up their security protection.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: TikTok is back on the App Store and Google Play Store – here’s what could happen next
item.description: TikTok has reappeared on the iOS App Store and Google Play Store following its banning – but the platform might not stick around for too long
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo
item.description: Grupo Piñero optimiza la experiencia del cliente a nivel tecnológico, operativo y participativo. Se apoya para ello en la plataforma unificada de gestión de la experiencia del cliente de Sprinklr.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Avoiding the cloud migration graveyard
item.description: Fictional global retailer StyleHaven embarked on its cloud migration journey, eager to modernize its systems and improve its customers’ experience. StyleHaven opted to “lift and shift,” assuming its existing infrastructure would transfer to the cloud with few issues. The result? Chaos.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: TikTok returns to Apple, Google app stores amid Trump ban delay
item.description: TikTok returned to the Apple and Google app stores Thursday evening after President Trump delayed the ban on the video-sharing app last month. The Chinese-owned app was removed from the stores after it briefly shut down in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk’s Tesla fined for alleged ‘serious’ failure to protect Fremont plant workers from excessive heat
item.description: The regulatory action comes as Tesla CEO Musk works as a special government employee for President Donald Trump to dramatically -- and critics say illegally -- slash federal government spending and programs, including in agencies responsible for regulating the operations of Tesla.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: 14 states sue Musk and Trump, calling billionaire's role unconstitutional
item.description: Fourteen states sued Elon Musk, his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) and President Trump on Thursday, arguing that the tech billionaire’s sweeping efforts to cut government spending are unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Bristling at 'Gulf of Mexico' name change on maps, Mexico says it might sue Google
item.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says her government is not ruling out filing a civil lawsuit against Google if it maintains its stance of calling the stretch of sea between northeastern Mexico and the southeastern United States the “Gulf of America.”.
item.dc:creator: Fabiola Sânchez, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fox host: Musk needs to be transparent about 'billions' in government contract
item.description: Fox News host Jessica Tarlov knocked billionaire Elon Musk over what she said is a lack of transparency he is displaying amid efforts to make widespread changes to the size of the federal government on President Trump's behalf.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, The Hill

item.title: The Rise of Advanced Malware in Mobile Apps
item.description: A new malware campaign has been detected, targeting mobile users with advanced info-stealing capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Innovators gear up work on ‘green’ hydrogen plane with plans for nonstop 9-day trip around Earth
item.description: Swiss aviation pioneer Bertrand Piccard is developing a plane powered by liquid hydrogen after a much-hyped flight in 2015 that used sunlight to travel around the world.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten And Mustakim Hasnath, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Italian government denies it spied on journalists and migrant activists using Paragon spyware
item.description: ROME (AP) — The Italian government denied Wednesday that it had spied on journalists and migrant activists using spyware but said it would cooperate with an investigation into “vulnerabilities” after at least seven Italian cellphones were apparently hacked with military-grade surveillance technology
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and PAOLO SANTALUCIA, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: The Memo: Musk rivals Trump as most controversial figure on political stage
item.description: President Trump may be only the second most controversial person in his own administration. Three weeks into Trump’s second term, Elon Musk has taken center stage — as big of a villain for liberals and Trump critics as he is a hero for MAGA conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, The Hill

item.title: Thomson Reuters scores early win in AI copyright battles in the US
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Thomson Reuters has won an early battle in court over the question of fair use in artificial intelligence-related copyright cases.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order
item.description: Ilona Cohen, the chief legal and policy officer at HackerOne, explains why President Joe Biden’s new cyber executive order should be a priority. The post Why the Trump administration should uphold this final cyber executive order first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Congress gets set to deal with artificial intelligence in health care
item.description: "AI is going to help kick in some of that load that the scientists and researchers do at the front end to quickly get a diagnosis," said Deema Tarazi. The post Congress gets set to deal with artificial intelligence in health care first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Latino workers working to overcome a technological divide brought on by automation, AI
item.description: As jobs become more reliant on technology some Latino workers can be left behind due to a lack of digital skills exacerbated by a lack of accessibility.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Is PSN still down? PlayStation Network addressing ongoing outage
item.description: Thousands of users have reported outages with the PlayStation Network since Friday evening but as of Saturday morning things still didn't seem back normal.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, NewsNation
